events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll write the required JSON directly to `/app/answer.json`. The condition is a clear compatibility/listing failure: aluminum conductors terminated on a device not rated for aluminum/CO-ALR use.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && printf '%s
' '{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"high\",
  \"work_stage\": \"rough-complete\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 80,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.93,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Aluminum conductor is terminated on a device that is not identified or rated CO/ALR for aluminum conductors.\",
    \"Termination is not compliant with conductor/device listing and creates elevated overheating and connection-failure risk.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"De-energize affected circuit until corrected if energized or capable of being energized.\",
    \"Replace the device with one listed and marked for aluminum conductor termination or provide an approved copper pigtail/connector method listed for AL-to-CU transition.\",
    \"Verify conductor size, insulation temperature rating, ampacity, terminal temperature rating, and torque per manufacturer instructions before inspection.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"Fail: even if the conductor ampacity is adequate under NEC 310 and Table 310.16, aluminum conductors must terminate only on equipment identified/listed for aluminum use. A device not rated CO/ALR is outside tolerance because the rough-in includes an incompatible installed termination, which can overheat or loosen under load.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"NEC 110.3(B) - listed/labeled equipment installed per instructions\",
    \"NEC 110.14 - electrical connections and conductor material compatibility\",
    \"NEC 310 - conductors for general wiring\",
    \"NEC Table 310.16 - allowable ampacities of insulated conductors\"
  ]
}' > /app/answer.json
python -m json.tool /app/answer.json"
